subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which monetary policy tool is primarily used by the RBI to absorb excess liquidity from the banking system on a short-term basis?

explanation.concept: Reverse Repo Rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from commercial banks to absorb excess liquidity.
step_by_step: Identify tool used to absorb liquidity.
Higher reverse repo encourages banks to park funds with RBI.

why_correct.B: Correct tool used to absorb liquidity.

why_incorrect.A: Repo rate injects liquidity, not absorbs.
why_incorrect.C: CRR is a reserve requirement, not short-term absorption tool.
why_incorrect.D: SLR is statutory requirement, not active liquidity tool.
